title: Gifts or donations
description: How to claim gifts or donations when you lodge your return using myTax.
keywords: gifts, donations, 2015 tax return, tax, return, 2015

content_blocks.text: Did you make a gift or donation of $2 or more to an approved organisation?
Your receipt should show whether your donation is tax-deductible.
To check whether an organisation is an approved organisation, contact them or go to abn.business.gov.au External Link
Generally, you cannot claim a deduction for a gift or donation if you received something in return (for example, raffle tickets or a dinner).

If you made one or more donations of $2 or more to bucket collections conducted by an approved organisation for natural disaster victims, you can claim a tax deduction of up to $10 for the total of those contributions without a receipt.
Read Gifts or donations if you need any more information or had any of the following:

items: Add up the amounts of all gifts and donations you are entitled to claim.

content_text:
## Did you make a gift or donation of $2 or more to an approved organisation?
Your receipt should show whether your donation is tax-deductible.
To check whether an organisation is an approved organisation, contact them or go to abn.business.gov.au External Link
Generally, you cannot claim a deduction for a gift or donation if you received something in return (for example, raffle tickets or a dinner).
## Answering this question
If you made one or more donations of $2 or more to bucket collections conducted by an approved organisation for natural disaster victims, you can claim a tax deduction of up to $10 for the total of those contributions without a receipt.
Read Gifts or donations if you need any more information or had any of the following:
- gifts of property and shares
- contributions to fund-raising events
- political donations.
## Completing your tax return
- Add up the amounts of all gifts and donations you are entitled to claim.
- Enter the total at 'Amount'.
- Click on 'Save'.
